_meta.purpose: Front-end/design INTENT registry for sidecoach-keyword.sh. The verb/mode registries fire on explicit sidecoach vocabulary (polish, forge, ...). This third tier fires on NATURAL front-end/design requests that contain no sidecoach verb, injecting a light, advisory self-question ('would sidecoach help here?') rather than a hard route. The goal is to ingrain sidecoach into the default evaluation of front-end work without bogging down trivial edits.
_meta.fire_rule: After no verb/mode match and outside the cooldown window, the intent nudge fires when (a strong build/design ACTION co-occurs with a SUBSTANTIVE_TARGET) OR (any STANDALONE signal appears). Informational framings (what is X / how to X / explain X / define X) are suppressed by the same logic the verb tier uses.
_meta.exempt_rule: A trivial-modify framing (EXEMPT) with no NEW_BUILD signal and no STANDALONE signal stays silent - it is a tweak, not design work. Minor targets (button, icon, color, font, label) are deliberately NOT in SUBSTANTIVE_TARGETS, so they never fire alone.
_meta.cooldown_rule: On ANY sidecoach engagement (verb route, mode route, or intent nudge) the hook touches config.cooldown_state_file. Intent nudges are suppressed while that file is younger than config.cooldown_seconds, so an active build and its follow-up tweaks are not re-nagged. Explicit verb/mode routes are NEVER suppressed by cooldown. Override seconds with env SIDECOACH_INTENT_COOLDOWN and the state-file path with env SIDECOACH_INTENT_COOLDOWN_FILE (used by the test harness).
_meta.pattern_syntax: Values are regex FRAGMENTS, matched case-insensitively with hyphen-aware word boundaries (?<![\w-]){pattern}(?![\w-]). Use [- ]? for optional space/hyphen and (?:a|b) for alternation. Tune freely - this file is the single source of truth for intent triggering.

nudge: This prompt reads as front-end / design work. Before hand-coding OR hand-eyeballing, ask: would a sidecoach flow produce a stronger result here? If you are BUILDING UI, sidecoach owns strategy, tokens, typography, references, and motion. If you are being asked to DIAGNOSE, review, or critique existing UI (for example 'what's wrong with this page', 'this feels off', 'look at <url>'), that IS a sidecoach audit - sidecoach's primary read path, NOT 'upstream of' it: run /sidecoach audit <target> as the FIRST step, before forming an opinion. It RENDERS the page and runs the detection engine (contrast, heading order, broken images, justified text, tiny-text, nested-cards, marketing-buzzword, anti-pattern bans) - the objective and taste defects a freeform eyeball read misses. It does NOT require a pending build or change; running it when nothing is being built is NOT dressing up an opinion as a formal pass - the freeform read is the opinion, the audit is the measurement. Use it for substantive UI work; skip it for genuinely trivial tweaks. If a hero or section wants a generative or shader background, sidecoach's tilt-lab capability (the /tilt-lab skill) can supply one. This is a self-evaluation prompt, not a mandate: decide, then proceed.
